message:
refactor(core): continue runtime_compat migration in snapshot_engine, distributed, and workflows

Advance the ongoing tokio → runtime_compat abstraction migration across
three modules, reducing direct tokio coupling for future executor
portability:

snapshot_engine.rs (+57/-57):
- Replace `tokio::sync::{Mutex, mpsc}` imports with
  `crate::runtime_compat::{Mutex, RwLock, mpsc, sleep, watch}`
- Migrate `session_id` and `last_state_hash` fields from
  `tokio::sync::RwLock` to `crate::runtime_compat::RwLock`
- Replace all 20 instances of `tokio::sync::watch::channel` in tests
  with `watch::channel`
- Update `run_periodic` signature from
  `tokio::sync::watch::Receiver<bool>` to `watch::Receiver<bool>`

distributed.rs (+12/-12):
- Replace `tokio::time::{Duration, timeout}` with `std::time::Duration`
  + `crate::runtime_compat::timeout`
- Update four TLS handshake test functions to use
  `crate::runtime_compat::timeout` instead of `tokio::time::timeout`

workflows.rs (+9/-4):
- Move `elapsed_ms(start: Instant) -> u64` helper from wezterm.rs into
  workflows.rs as a local utility (it was only used here after the
  previous runtime_compat migration removed the wezterm re-export)
- Consolidate `use std::time::{Duration, Instant}` into a single import
  and remove the now-redundant `use std::time::Instant` at module tail

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
